name: repo-review-before-merge
summary: Pre-merge review pass — correctness, tests, security, style, docs. Can be solo or multi-agent (via /cc-council).
when_to_use: Before any PR merges to protected branches. Especially main/master.

phases.name: Context
steps: Read the diff in full (not just changed-file names).
Identify the blast radius: what consumers of the changed code exist?

phases.name: Correctness
steps: Does the change do what the PR description claims?
Any off-by-one, null/undefined, async race conditions?
Any state mutation that could leak across requests/users?

phases.name: Tests
steps: New tests cover the new behavior?
Existing tests still make sense (not just tweaked to pass)?
Test data doesn't leak real secrets?

phases.name: Security
steps: Any new external input? Is it validated/sanitized?
Any auth/authz changes? Checked on the right boundary?
Any secrets in diff (keys, tokens, credentials)?

phases.name: Style
steps: Consistent with existing patterns in the codebase?
Any dead code or commented-out blocks?

phases.name: Docs
steps: If public API changed: reference docs updated?
If config schema changed: settings docs updated?

exit_criteria: Either [OK] with green across all phases, or a list of [BLOCK]/[NIT] findings with specific file:line references.
anti_patterns: Only reading the diff without repo context — misses cross-cutting impact.
Nit-only reviews — let real bugs through.
